2018-03-25.title: A First in Flight: Australia to the U.K., in 17 Hours
2018-03-25.abstract: Qantas calls its first direct, nonstop Dreamliner flight from Perth to London a “game changer.”

2018-03-26.title: Trump and Western Allies Expel Scores of Russians in Sweeping Rebuke Over U.K. Poisoning
2018-03-26.abstract: The expulsion order included 12 people identified as Russian intelligence officers stationed at the United Nations. The Russian consulate in Seattle also must close.

2018-03-26.title: Live in a Drainpipe? Five Extreme Ideas to Solve Hong Kong’s Housing Crisis
2018-03-26.abstract: With little available land and housing prices soaring ever higher, Hong Kong is considering some audacious ways to house residents.

2018-03-26.title: Astonishing Admission of Cheating Rocks Australian Cricket
2018-03-26.abstract: Senior players schemed to tamper with the ball during a series in South Africa, a revelation that has stunned a sport that prides itself on fair play.

2018-03-26.title: U.S.-Russia Relations: How Latest Blowup Compares to Earlier Ones
2018-03-26.abstract: The expulsion of Russian officials in response to the alleged poisoning of a former spy resembles the tactics of the Cold War.

2018-03-26.title: Expelling Russians, Europe and North America Show Unity With U.K.
2018-03-26.abstract: The expulsion of dozens of diplomats from Europe, and dozens more across the Atlantic, came weeks after the nerve agent poisoning of a former Russian spy in Britain.

2018-03-27.title: Trump Fund-Raiser Files Hacking Lawsuit Against Qatar
2018-03-27.abstract: Leaked emails suggest that Elliott Broidy promised foreign leaders access to the Trump administration. His suit accuses Qatar of being behind the leak.

2018-03-27.title: Scores of Russians Expelled by U.S. and Its Allies Over U.K. Poisoning
2018-03-27.abstract: The mass expulsion of Russian personnel in the United States was the largest ever, yet the president avoided any public condemnation of Russia’s role in an attack in Britain.

2018-03-27.title: What Kim Jong-un May Get in Reaching Out to China
2018-03-27.abstract: In an unexpected burst of diplomacy, North Korea’s leader may be playing the role of international statesman while courting an ally that was losing patience with his country.

2018-03-27.title: Bulletproof, Slow and Full of Wine: Kim Jong-un’s Mystery Train
2018-03-27.abstract: In earlier incarnations, the North Korean leader’s train has featured sumptuous feasts, cases of fine French wines and female entertainers known as lady conductors.

2018-03-27.title: Taliban’s Rare Silence on Talks Charges Up a New Peace Conference
2018-03-27.abstract: A sweeping Afghan offer to bring the insurgents to the table has led to a hint of optimism among diplomats, and internal discussion by Taliban leaders.

2018-03-28.title: How Viktor Orban Bends Hungarian Society to His Will
2018-03-28.abstract: To populists like Stephen Bannon, Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orban, is “a hero.” But democracy advocates fear his relentless remolding of society.

2018-03-28.title: Welcome to the Neighborhood
2018-03-28.abstract: After enduring the struggle to buy a home, a multicultural crowd of friends gathers for a Hindu celebration.

2018-03-28.title: China Moves to Sideline Underground Bishop, His Colleagues Say
2018-03-28.abstract: Guo Xijin was taken to the city of Xiamen after being briefly detained, and members of his staff said he was expected to resume his forced vacation later this week.

2018-03-28.title: Myanmar Picks a New President, but He’ll Still Be No. 2
2018-03-28.abstract: Chosen by Parliament, U Win Myint is largely expected to do the bidding of Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, who is not allowed to hold the presidency herself.

2018-03-28.title: El Al Sues Israel After Air India Flies Through Saudi Airspace
2018-03-28.abstract: Air India became the first commercial airline permitted to fly over Saudi Arabia en route to and from Israel. Claiming discrimination, El Al is suing.

2018-03-29.title: Venezuela Jail Fire Killed at Least 68, Attorney General Says
2018-03-29.abstract: The announcement came after crowds of relatives gathered outside the facility, some weeping and others facing off with police officers in riot gear.

2018-03-29.title: North and South Korea Set a Date for Summit Meeting at Border
2018-03-29.abstract: Kim Jong-un and South Korea’s president, Moon Jae-in, will hold talks on April 27 at the so-called truce village of Panmunjom, officials said.

2018-03-29.title: Malala Yousafzai Returns to Pakistan for First Time Since Attack
2018-03-29.abstract: Ms. Yousafzai, a Nobel laureate studying at Oxford, met with the prime minister and will visit a school she helped build near her native Swat Valley.

2018-03-29.title: India Spurns the Dalai Lama’s Celebration, Worried About China
2018-03-29.abstract: With tense meetings ahead with Beijing, Indian officials have been ordered to stay away from the Dalai Lama’s rally thanking India for hosting his exile.

2018-03-29.title: Mick Fanning, a Hero of Australian Surfing, Paddles Out One Last Time
2018-03-29.abstract: Mick Fanning, a three-time world champion whose list of accomplishments includes punching a great white shark, is preparing his exit from pro surfing.

2018-03-30.title: Greece’s Island of Despair
2018-03-30.abstract: Two years after the E.U. and Turkey struck a deal to cut off asylum seekers crossing the Aegean, thousands languish in deplorable conditions on Lesbos.

2018-03-30.title: Thailand Bus Fire Kills 20 Migrant Workers From Myanmar
2018-03-30.abstract: The workers, who were headed to a factory district near Bangkok, had completed a difficult new registration process to work in Thailand.

2018-03-30.title: Where the Genocide Museum Is (Mostly) Mum on the Fate of Jews
2018-03-30.abstract: The Lithuanian capital is divided on how to face its own messy history, amid accusations of 21st-century Holocaust denial. Was the Soviet occupation really “genocide”?

2018-03-30.title: Can North Korea Handle a K-Pop Invasion?
2018-03-30.abstract: Red Velvet and other acts will perform in Pyongyang this weekend, the first appearance by South Korean artists in the North for more than a decade.

2018-03-30.title: Trump Orders State Dept. to Suspend Funds for Syria Recovery
2018-03-30.abstract: The move came as two members of the United States-led coalition fighting in Syria were killed and five others were wounded by a bomb in a late-night attack.

2018-03-31.title: Russia’s Feared ‘Night Wolves’ Bike Gang Came to Bosnia. Bosnia Giggled.
2018-03-31.abstract: A Kremlin-funded tour by a biker gang known as Putin’s Angels, agents of meddling and mayhem, stoked fears about what Russia is up to in Bosnia.

2018-03-31.title: U.N. Unveils Blacklist to Fight North Korea Sanctions-Busting
2018-03-31.abstract: The Security Council targeted 27 ships and 21 shipping companies, in what a U.S. official called its largest ever such move against the North.

2018-03-31.title: Saving a Fussy Predator in Europe, With Help From 50,000 Rabbits
2018-03-31.abstract: After nearing extinction 20 years ago, the Iberian lynx has made a comeback in southern Spain and Portugal, thanks to a vast recovery program funded by the European Union.

2018-03-31.title: ‘2 Bitter Options’ for Syrians Trapped Between Assad and Extremists
2018-03-31.abstract: With no other choice, tens of thousands of Syrians have fled to Idlib, a rebel-held province, where they are caught between government attacks from the sky and Islamists on the ground.

2018-03-31.title: Denmark Gets First Public Statue of a Black Woman, a ‘Rebel Queen’
2018-03-31.abstract: As a challenge to the country’s collective memory, two artists unveiled a monument to a 19th-century figure who led a fiery revolt against Danish colonial rule in the Caribbean.
